topic: Spelling Nouns: Changing Adjectives with -ance/-ancy and -ence/-ency
title: Making Your Words Sound Formal: Adding -ance, -ence, -ancy, -ency
explanation_chunks: When you write arguments, using more formal language can make your writing sound more grown-up and convincing. One way to do this is by changing adjectives (describing words) into nouns (naming words).
Many adjectives that end in '-ant' can be changed into nouns by adding '-ance' or '-ancy'. It helps to remember which ending goes with which word.
Similarly, adjectives that end in '-ent' can often become nouns by adding '-ence' or '-ency'. It's all about learning these special endings to make your sentences sound more formal and precise.
examples: Instead of saying 'It is important that children can access education,' which uses the adjective 'important', you can say 'It is of great **importance** that children can access education.' The word 'importance' is a noun.
If someone is 'hesitant' (an adjective meaning unsure), you can talk about their 'hesitancy' (a noun meaning being unsure).
Someone who is 'intelligent' (an adjective describing cleverness) shows 'intelligence' (a noun referring to cleverness).
key_points: Formal writing often uses nouns instead of adjectives.
Adjectives ending in '-ant' often change to nouns with '-ance' or '-ancy'.
Adjectives ending in '-ent' often change to nouns with '-ence' or '-ency'.
Learning these patterns helps with spelling and making your writing more sophisticated.

vocabulary.definition: unsure or slow to act or speak
the state of being hesitant or unsure
having or showing intelligence, especially of a high level
the ability to learn and apply knowledge and skills
the quality or state of being urgent; a need for immediate action
not making or accompanied by any sound

explain_again: Sometimes, we want our words to sound a bit more grown-up. This page teaches you how to change 'describing words' (like 'important') into 'naming words' (like 'importance') by adding special endings like '-ance' or '-ence'. It's like giving your words a fancy new job title!
fun_analogy: Imagine words are like LEGO bricks. We're learning how to snap on new special end-bricks (-ance, -ence) to change a 'describing brick' into a 'naming brick', building more complex and formal sentences!
